J.R.R. Tolkien's epic story is brought to life masterfully by Jackson. Bolstered by stunning New Zealand landscapes, this sprawling adventure mixes world-building, intense action, and themes of courage and friendship. Though it is very long, the story is captivating - it is a movie made for the big screen, and that is where it's best experienced. It is also worth adding that despite the movie's CGI being 25 years old, there are very few moments that look obviously like CGI. A timeless triumph of storytelling and spectacle.

I have seen this movie in the theater about five times over the last two and a half decades. It is amazing, especially considering how long ago it was made. I believe part of the best trilogy ever film.

Without a doubt, the best trilogy ever created, and it will never be surpassed. It was made at the perfect time, with the right people, and in the perfect historical context. Even with some liberties taken from the original work, they contribute to and enhance the film's visual appeal. All its scenes are believable and epic, immersing us in the greatest fantasy world ever created. Thank you for existing.

One ring that controls all other rings, Frodo is tasked to destroy it in Mordor while being hunted down by the ring's owner Sauron and his Ringwraiths. Frodo gathers a fellowship as Saruman starts raising an army of Orcs incubating in the mud to fight under Sauron. An epic plot to set one of the best trilogies in motion with great battles and a Balrog!
